rules.description: AI-overused vocabulary, 2023 to mid-2024 (GPT-4) bucket. Scored by density and co-occurrence, never by a single hit: one or two hits may be coincidence, but these words cluster. 'key' is matched only in its importance-adjective position to spare 'API key' and 'XOR key'.
rules.source_citation: Wikipedia:Signs of AI writing § Words overused by AI, 2023 to mid-2024 bucket

positive: The report delves into an intricate tapestry of tradecraft, a testament to the evolving landscape.
negative: The malware reads the XOR key from its config and decrypts the API key store.

rules.description: AI-overused vocabulary, mid-2024 to mid-2025 (GPT-4o) bucket. Overlaps the GPT-4 bucket on purpose because the guide's lists overlap; the era estimator disambiguates on distinctive terms.
rules.source_citation: Wikipedia:Signs of AI writing § Words overused by AI, mid-2024 to mid-2025 bucket

positive: This approach aligns with best practices, fostering resilience and showcasing the team's depth while highlighting key wins.
negative: The patch changed the parser. Nothing else moved.

rules.description: AI-overused vocabulary, mid-2025 onward (GPT-5) bucket: the list narrows to emphasizing, enhance, highlighting, showcasing. Notability-claim language is covered by the canned-notability category. Era estimator calls gpt5 only when this narrowed set fires without earlier-era distinctive terms.
rules.source_citation: Wikipedia:Signs of AI writing § Words overused by AI, mid-2025 onward bucket

positive: Enhancing coverage while showcasing results and highlighting the approach, emphasizing outcomes.
negative: The actor pivoted to a second C2 within an hour.

rules.description: Grok-idiosyncratic vocabulary: causal, empirical, correlate, plus continued overuse of underscore as of 2026.
rules.source_citation: Wikipedia:Signs of AI writing § Words overused by AI, Grok notes

positive: The empirical data correlate with a causal link, underscoring the empirical trend.
negative: The beacon interval was 300 seconds with 20 percent jitter.
